ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry exists for Laksa Pizza specifically. FOODON_00002044 (pizza food product) is used as the closest parent category. The laksa-spiced coconut curry topping is a Singaporean fusion element not represented in FoodOn.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Pizza) is used as the closest parent. No specific code exists for Laksa Pizza or Singaporean fusion pizza variants in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept exists for Laksa Pizza or a sufficiently close fusion pizza category.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Laksa Pizza serving is approximately 350 g, equivalent to a personal-sized or shared pizza of roughly 8 slices, as typically served at a Singaporean fusion restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from component analysis: pizza base (USDA SR Legacy, Item 21301 'Pizza, cheese, regular crust') and laksa sauce components (HPB Singapore Foodo, Laksa; coconut milk per USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 168351)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for Laksa Pizza exists in any approved primary source. Values are estimated by combining a standard cheese pizza base (USDA SR Legacy Item 21301, per 100 g: energy 266 kcal, protein 11.4 g, fat 10.4 g, carbohydrate 33.6 g, sodium 598 mg) with an adjustment for laksa-style coconut curry sauce and seafood toppings, referencing HPB Foodo laksa nutrient data and USDA coconut milk data. The resulting composite estimate reflects a moderately rich, spiced pizza with elevated fat and sodium relative to a plain cheese pizza. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of pizza estimated from analogous food: 'Pizza, Super Supreme (Pizza Hut)' GI = 36 and 'White bread pizza base' GI ~60–65; composite estimate anchored to standard pizza base GI values from the University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Laksa Pizza. The University of Sydney GI Database lists pizza variants with GI values ranging from 36 (Pizza Hut Super Supreme, thick crust) to approximately 60–65 for standard white-flour pizza bases. Given that Laksa Pizza uses a standard wheat-flour pizza base with a moderately rich coconut-curry sauce (fat content slows gastric emptying), a GI of 60 is used as a conservative mid-range estimate consistent with a medium-GI pizza. Glycemic load per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (60 × 103.25) / 100 ≈ 61.9, where carbohydrate per 350 g serving = 29.5 × 3.5 = 103.25 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~620 mg per 100 g; ~2170 mg per 350 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Laksa Pizza consumption due to its high sodium load from laksa paste, fish sauce, and processed cheese, which can significantly contribute to or exceed the recommended daily sodium intake of 2000 mg in a single serving.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (~60) and high glycemic load per serving (~61.9) from refined wheat flour pizza base
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume Laksa Pizza in small portions as the refined wheat base produces a moderate glycaemic response, and the high glycaemic load per full serving can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; pairing with a fibre-rich salad and limiting portion to 1–2 slices is advisable.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (~6.2 g per 100 g) from coconut milk and cheese, combined with high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit Laksa Pizza intake due to its combination of saturated fat from coconut milk and mozzarella cheese and high sodium from laksa paste, both of which are associated with adverse cardiovascular outcomes when consumed in excess.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Laksa Pizza is a Singaporean fusion dish that combines the bold, aromatic flavours of laksa — a spiced coconut curry noodle soup iconic to Singapore and Malaysia — with the format of a Western-style baked pizza. The pizza base is a standard wheat-flour dough, topped with a rich laksa-spiced coconut milk sauce in place of conventional tomato sauce, then layered with mozzarella cheese, prawns, fish cake slices, bean sprouts, and finished with laksa leaves (Vietnamese coriander) and sliced chilli. The result is a dish with a crispy-chewy base, a creamy and fragrant coconut-curry sauce, and the characteristic spicy, lemongrass-and-galangal aroma of Singaporean laksa. Nutritionally, Laksa Pizza is moderately energy-dense (~272 kcal per 100 g) with a notable fat contribution from coconut milk and cheese (total fat ~12.5 g/100 g, saturated fat ~6.2 g/100 g) and a high sodium content (~620 mg/100 g) from laksa paste and processed ingredients. The glycaemic index is estimated at approximately 60 (medium), and a full serving carries a high glycaemic load. Clinically, this dish warrants caution for individuals managing hypertension (due to sodium), Type 2 Diabetes (due to refined carbohydrate load), and cardiovascular disease (due to saturated fat and sodium). It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those with shellfish, fish, gluten, or dairy allergies. Laksa Pizza is a creative restaurant innovation reflecting Singapore's multicultural culinary identity, blending Peranakan-Malay laksa heritage with global pizza culture.

confidence_reason: No direct entry for Laksa Pizza exists in any approved primary nutrient database. Nutrient values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy cheese pizza data with HPB Foodo laksa sauce data and USDA coconut milk data. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database pizza entries. Medium confidence reflects the composite estimation approach and the absence of a direct laboratory analysis for this specific fusion dish.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates; no single approved source covers Laksa Pizza directly. Values should be updated if a direct laboratory analysis or HPB Foodo entry becomes available. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used for this estimate (USDA SR Legacy pizza base and HPB Foodo laksa). 3. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used for this estimate. 4. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used for this estimate. 5. Halal status is listed as false because laksa paste formulations vary and some commercial versions may contain non-halal ingredients; consumers should verify with the specific restaurant. 6. GI is estimated at 60 (medium) based on analogous pizza entries from the University of Sydney GI Database; this is a conservative estimate acknowledging that fat from coconut milk may lower the effective GI somewhat. 7. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (60 × 103.25) / 100 ≈ 61.9, where 103.25 g carbohydrate is derived from 29.5 g/100 g × 350 g serving size.
